chunk_summary: Hygiene counseling for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants focuses on safe formula preparation, breast milk handling, and feeding equipment cleaning to prevent infections and ensure growth stability post-discharge.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of hygiene in preventing infections and diarrheal illness in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Learn safe preparation and storage practices for powdered infant formula and expressed breast milk.
Identify appropriate feeding equipment and cleaning protocols for home feeding.
Recognize common hygiene errors and their potential medical risks.
key_facts: Powdered infant formula is not sterile; reconstitute with water at ~70°C to reduce bacterial risk.
Do not store reconstituted formula at room temperature or reuse leftover milk after a feed.
Breast milk should be labeled with the time expressed and discarded if kept beyond the safe window for the local context.
Cup/spoon/paladai feeding is safer than bottles in many settings due to easier cleaning and reduced biofilm formation.
Families using bottles must demonstrate proper cleaning and sterilization routines.
Avoid adding honey, sugar, ghee, cereal water, or unprescribed powders to milk feeds.
reference_quote: Powdered infant formula is not sterile. Use safe water for reconstitution and clean, boiled utensils. When feasible, preparing formula with hot water (about 70°C) reduces bacterial risk. (Source: WHO)
document_key_topics: Safe preparation and storage of infant formula
